title: G1: Edge-on degeneracy of the velocity decomposition must be proved quantitatively

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap (conceptual core).** The statement claims that the observed LOS rotation signal of the GPoA could be a *projected, edge-on* artifact rather than a true rotating plane. A correct solution must PROVE this, not assert it.

**What to show.** Let $\hat s$ be the MW$\to$satellite LOS unit vector and $\hat n$ the GPoA normal. The observed quantity is the LOS speed $v_{\rm los}=\mathbf v\cdot\hat s$; the quantity of interest is the perpendicular speed $v_\perp=\mathbf v\cdot\hat n$. 'Near-edge-on' means $\hat s\cdot\hat n\approx 0$. Decompose $\hat s = (\hat s\cdot\hat n)\,\hat n + \hat s_\parallel$ with $\hat s_\parallel$ in-plane; then
$$v_{\rm los} = (\hat s\cdot\hat n)\,v_\perp + \mathbf v_\parallel\cdot\hat s_\parallel.$$
Since $\hat s\cdot\hat n=\cos\theta$ with $\theta\approx 90^\circ$, the perpendicular speed enters $v_{\rm los}$ only at order $\cos\theta\ll 1$, while in-plane rotation enters at order unity. Hence LOS coherence is consistent with EITHER true in-plane rotation OR large $v_\perp$, and only the transverse (proper-motion) channel breaks the degeneracy.

**Requirement.** Define all reference frames explicitly, compute the actual edge-on angle $\theta$ for the GPoA, and give the quantitative suppression factor $\cos\theta$. This justifies why proper motions are mathematically necessary.
